title: Polestar Reports Revenue Growth 56 Percent First Half 2025
text: Polestar reported H1 2025 earnings showing 56% revenue growth with strong delivery performance. EPS negative $0.56 while revenue reached $1.4 billion beating revenue estimate. Retail sales rose 51% to over 30,000 cars with 56% revenue growth. Challenges included GAAP gross margin of negative 49% after $739 million impairment on Polestar 3 assets, though adjusted gross margin improved to plus 1.4% year-over-year. Full year 2025 showed retail sales of estimated 15,608 cars in Q4, up 27% versus Q4 2024 with full year totalling approximately 60,119 cars, 34% growth versus 2024. Management guidance reiterates 30-35% CAGR retail growth target for 2025-2027 without issuing financial guidance at time. Key metrics showed 230 retail sales points in operation versus 154 at end of Q1 2025, representing 50% growth. Company benefitted from growing share of higher-priced Polestar 3 and Polestar 4 models in product mix, though tariffs and pricing pressure subsequently emerged as challenges.
